refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall maintain teleoperation link availability at or above the customer-defined target over the agreed evaluation period.
refined_fr.rationale: Refining the original FR to explicitly state the performance metric (link availability) and the evaluation period clarifies the requirement for design and verification.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure link uptime statistics over the evaluation period and compare against the customer-defined availability target.
sub_frs: FR1: Monitor teleoperation link status continuously.
FR2: Detect and recover from link failures promptly.
FR3: Report link availability metrics to stakeholders.

candidate_dps: DP1: Implement redundant network links with automatic failover.
DP2: Use link health monitoring and predictive failure detection algorithms.
DP3: Provide real-time link status reporting and alerting.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Implement redundant network links with automatic failover.

coupling_check.notes: Redundant network links logically isolate link availability concerns, minimizing coupling with other system functions.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network infrastructure supports redundancy and failover.
Risk of increased system complexity impacting maintainability.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating DP2 and DP3 in subsequent refinement levels for enhanced monitoring and reporting.

im_boundary.name: Vehicle
im_boundary.scope: Physical network hardware and embedded link management software within the vehicle.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Interface for redundant network connections to support failover.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Reporting link status and availability metrics to NGHE Solution.

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment as link hardware and failover logic reside here; low diffusion as functionality is localized.
Partial containment for link usage but no direct control over redundancy.
System-level monitoring but no direct link control.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Assigning link availability DP to Vehicle module centralizes control and minimizes diffusion, improving maintainability and clarity.
containment_risks: Potential hardware failure modes may require cross-module coordination.
Software updates for failover logic must be carefully managed within Vehicle module.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Review interface definitions to ensure seamless integration with Teleoperation and NGHE Solution modules.

fr_dp_gate.reason: Selected DP (redundant network links with failover) directly addresses the refined FR and is logically decoupled.

diffusion_risks: Low diffusion risk as DP is contained within Vehicle module.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores support Vehicle as the best IM boundary for this DP.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification via telemetry data and link status monitoring is feasible within assigned IM.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of responsibility to Vehicle module for link availability.

recommended_revisions: Consider adding monitoring and reporting DPs in next refinement level.
Clarify interface protocols for Network Link and Telemetry.

decision.justification: The current decomposition is sound but can be improved by explicitly including monitoring and reporting sub-FRs and corresponding DPs to fully cover link availability management.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Monitor teleoperation link status continuously.
FR2: Detect and recover from link failures promptly.
FR3: Report link availability metrics to stakeholders.

revise.why: To decompose the high-level FR into atomic sub-FRs that enable more precise DP and IM assignments.

assumptions: Vehicle module can host failover logic.
open_risks: Complexity of failover logic may impact maintainability.
Interface coordination with Teleoperation and NGHE Solution modules.
evidence_needed: Network performance data under redundancy scenarios.
Verification test plans for link availability measurement.
